title: Queen's Gambit Declined: Slav Defense
summary: Learn the solid Slav Defense, where Black declines the gambit and creates a firm pawn structure.
description: The Slav Defense is one of the most popular replies to the Queen's Gambit. Black declines the pawn but maintains a solid structure. This tutorial covers the main line, the exchange variation, and typical plans for both sides.

variations.exchange: 5.e3 - The Exchange Slav without early complications
variations.meran: 5.e4 - The Meran Variation, a sharp alternative

objectives: Understand the Slav structure and why it's so solid
Learn the typical pawn breaks: ...e6, ...c5, and ...b5
Master the piece development: where pieces go in the Slav
plans: White's plan: Build pressure on the c4 pawn and use the bishop pair
Black's plan: Play ...e6, ...Be7, and challenge the center with ...c5
The move ...b5 is Black's main queenside expansion
White should avoid exchanging too much - keep the pressure
commonMistakes: Do not think the Slav is boring - there are many tactical ideas
Avoid playing ...e6 too early - wait for the right moment
Do not underestimate Black's queenside play - the ...b5 break is powerful
White should maintain pressure - Black is solid but passive
completionSummary: The Slav Defense is a reliable weapon. You've learned the main ideas and can navigate the position.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Slav Defense is one of Black's most solid responses.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black responds in the center
steps.instruction: Black plays d5, challenging White's center.
steps.coaching: Black meets the Queen's Gambit with a solid central reply.

steps.title: The Queen's Gambit
steps.coaching: This is the Queen's Gambit, offering a pawn for development.

steps.title: The Slav move
steps.instruction: Black plays c6, declining the gambit.
steps.coaching: This is the Slav Defense! Black maintains a solid pawn structure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops naturally, controlling e5 and d4.

steps.title: Develop the other knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing and controlling d5.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, controlling the key d5 square.

steps.title: Black captures the pawn
steps.coaching: Black takes the pawn, entering the Slav structure.

steps.title: The a4 Slav
steps.coaching: This is the modern approach, preventing Black's typical break.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops the bishop, attacking e4.

steps.title: Support the pawn
steps.instruction: Play e3 to support the c4 pawn.
steps.coaching: White strengthens the position and prepares development.

steps.title: Black plays e6
steps.instruction: Black pushes e6, completing the Slav structure.
steps.coaching: Black adopts the typical Slav pawn structure: pawns on c6, e6, d5.

steps.title: Regain the pawn
steps.instruction: Play b3 to regain the c4 pawn.
steps.coaching: White regains the pawn while maintaining the center.

steps.title: Black exchanges
steps.coaching: Black exchanges, maintaining the solid structure.
